name: Deep-Fried Sage Leaves
recipeIngredient: Hot oil for deep frying (to a depth of 1 inch)
recipeInstructions:
1. Select whole, unblemished sage leaves with stems left on for easy handling.
2. Combine the beer, flour, salt, and pepper in a small bowl until smooth. The batter should have the consistency of cream soup. Allow it to stand at room temperature for at least 15 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, beat the egg white until it is fairly stiff but not dry. Gently fold the egg white into the batter.
4. Dip each sage leaf into the batter, coating both sides.
5. Deep-fry the coated sage leaves in hot oil or shortening until they are crisp and golden brown on both sides. Drain on paper towels before serving.
6. Serve with selected cheeses, or with deep-fried wedges of camembert or brie and a tomato dipping sauce.
Yield: 4 to 6 servings.
